response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned staff-level generalist with ~18 years of software engineering, strong TypeScript/Node.js depth, cloud experience (AWS CDK, Kubernetes), and recent hands-on work with MCP code generators and agentic-workflow infrastructure. However, the role requires 8+ years building production-grade AI solutions, and the candidate's demonstrable GenAI experience spans roughly one year, with no evidence of RAG architectures, vector databases, evaluation pipelines, LLM optimization, or Python — all core to this job. Customer-facing strengths (workshops, courses, conference talks) partially match the forward-deployed profile, but the AI depth gap is substantial.
strengths: Staff-level engineering experience across web, backend, embedded, and high-throughput distributed systems
Recent hands-on MCP and agentic-workflow tooling work directly relevant to the role's key technologies
Proven communicator: created multi-day courses, workshops, and conference talks, fitting the customer-facing mandate
concerns: Falls far short of the 8+ years required experience building production AI-driven solutions (~1 year of GenAI work)
No demonstrated experience with RAG, vector databases, LLM evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks like LangGraph/CrewAI
Python is absent from the skill set despite being a primary required language for the role

key_factors.factor: Insufficient production GenAI experience vs. 8-year requirement
Missing core AI stack (RAG, vector DBs, Python, agent frameworks)
Strong staff-level engineering breadth plus MCP/agentic tooling exposure
